---
title: "Mission"
---

## About the Program

Led by the University of New South Wales, the NSW Child E Cohort Program aims to inform policy and practice that supports better health, development, and social outcomes for NSW children — particularly disadvantaged children — across their early life.

Health and human services data have been linked for more than 2 million NSW children born since 2001, and their parents. This includes data from the health, education, justice, and social housing sectors, giving the Program a whole-of-population view of children's experiences and outcomes as they grow up.

## Our aims

- Build and maintain a linked, whole-of-population cohort of NSW children and their families.
- Understand the early-life factors that shape children's health, development, and wellbeing over time.
- Identify children and families who may benefit most from prevention and early intervention services.
- Translate findings into policy and practice that improves outcomes for NSW children.

## Approach

*[Placeholder — describe the Program's data sources, methods, and partnerships, e.g. linked administrative health, education, and welfare records; collaborations with NSW government agencies; approach to data governance and ethics.]*

## Funding & partners

*[Placeholder — list funders, partner agencies, and collaborating institutions.]*
